paper_title: Study on SCL Cleaning Methods and Pollen Adhesion Analysis

methods.method_name: SCL Cleaning and Pollen Adhesion Analysis
methods.description: Procedure for contaminating soft contact lenses (SCLs) with Japanese cedar pollen, cleaning them using different methods (saline vs. hydrogen peroxide), and analyzing the remaining pollen adhesion via microscopy and image analysis.

steps.description: Japanese cedar pollen collected on March 3, 2019, was used in this study (Yamizo Pollen Study Group, Daigo-cho, Ibaraki Prefecture, Japan).

unused SCLs (J & J 2 week Acuvue; Johnson & Johnson, Chiyoda-ku, Tokyo, Japan) were used. The details of SCL are as follows: FDA group (IV), principal components (2-HEMA, MAA), USAN nomenclature (etafilcon A), water content (58%), oxygen permeability (DK; 28.0×10 −11 (cm2/sec)(mlO2/mL×mmHg)), and oxygen transmissibility (Dk/L; 33.3×10 −9 (cm×mLO2/ sec×mL×mmHg).
steps.searchable_summary: Obtain unused soft contact lenses (SCLs) with specified characteristics.
inputs: SCL specifications (FDA group, components, water content, Dk, Dk/L)

steps.description: A drop of 0.2 mL of physiological saline containing 0.01 mg/mL of cedar pollen was dropped inside unused SCLs
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare physiological saline solution containing cedar pollen at a specified concentration.

steps.description: A drop of 0.2 mL of physiological saline containing 0.01 mg/mL of cedar pollen was dropped inside unused SCLs
steps.searchable_summary: Apply pollen-saline suspension inside unused SCLs.

steps.description: and kept at room temperature for 1 hour.
steps.searchable_summary: Incubate contaminated SCLs at room temperature for 1 hour.

steps.description: After 1 hour, the SCLs were randomly divided into two groups, the saline group (n = 10) and the hydrogen peroxide group (n = 10)
steps.searchable_summary: Randomly divide incubated SCLs into two groups: saline and hydrogen peroxide.

steps.description: and then the SCLs were rubbed and rinsed three times. The rubbing method was as follows: the lens was placed on the palm, a few drops of physiological saline were applied, and both sides of the lens were rubbed lightly with fingers 30 times. The SCLs were further rinsed with physiological saline.
steps.searchable_summary: Rub and rinse SCLs three times using physiological saline.
inputs: Rubbing method details (on palm, few drops saline, rub both sides, 30 times)

steps.description: The SCLs were placed in the lens holder of a disposable cup and were soaked with each chemical solution for 6 hours at room temperature according to the manufacturer's instructions.
steps.searchable_summary: Soak SCLs in assigned chemical solution (saline or hydrogen peroxide) for 6 hours at room temperature.
inputs: Assigned chemical solutions (physiological saline, hydrogen peroxide)
Soaking conditions (lens holder, disposable cup, 6 hours, room temperature, manufacturer's instructions)

steps.description: The SCLs were further rinsed three times with physiological saline.
steps.searchable_summary: Rinse SCLs three times with physiological saline after soaking.

steps.description: The central part of the inner surface of the SCL was observed under a microscope (BX-61, Olympus, Tokyo, Japan) attached with a CCD camera.
steps.searchable_summary: Observe the central part of the SCL inner surface using a microscope with a CCD camera.
outputs: Microscopic view of SCL inner surface

steps.description: And the images were first captured in high TIFF format with an original size of 4800x3600 pixels (width × height) in 24-bit red, green and blue (RGB) color at 72 pixels per inch resolution
steps.searchable_summary: Capture images of the observed area in high TIFF format with specified resolution.

steps.description: and transferred to a computer equipped with the image analysis software to be analyzed.
steps.searchable_summary: Transfer captured images to a computer for analysis.

steps.description: Additionally, the observed images were converted to the JPEG format for the analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Convert captured images from TIFF to JPEG format.

steps.description: The number of particles adhering to the area of 100 µm × 100 µm in the central part of the saved image (the central part of CL) and the ratio of the adhering area were analyzed using ImageJ analysis software (version 1.52a; Wayne Rasband, NIH, Bethesda, MD, http://imagej.nih.gov/ ij/docs/index. html).
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze images using ImageJ to count particles and measure adhering area ratio within a defined area.
inputs: JPEG images of SCL inner surface
outputs: Number of adhering particles per 100 µm × 100 µm area
Ratio of adhering area per 100 µm × 100 µm area
